operating_state: Total revenues increased to $36.1 billion in Q2 2021 from $15.9 billion in Q2 2020, driven by higher crude oil realizations and downstream margins following recovery from COVID-19 demand destruction. Upstream earnings recovered significantly from prior year losses due to absence of 2020 impairments and higher crude oil realizations. Operating cash flow reached $11.2 billion for the first six months with positive free cash flow of $7.6 billion versus negative $423 million in the prior year.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased to $4.9 billion from $5.5 billion year-over-year reflecting ongoing cost management and absence of prior year severance accruals. Depreciation declined significantly to $4.5 billion from $6.7 billion due to absence of prior year impairments. Purchased crude oil and products costs increased substantially consistent with higher commodity prices and increased throughput volumes. Capital expenditures totaled $5.3 billion for six months with full-year guidance reduced to approximately $13 billion.
material_events: Sanctioned the $4 billion Jansz-Io compression project in Australia and the Whale project in deepwater Gulf of Mexico. Completed acquisition of remaining Noble Midstream Partners units reducing noncontrolling interests. Resumed share repurchase program in July 2021 at expected rate of $2-3 billion annually after March 2020 suspension with $19.5 billion remaining under authorization. Began producing renewable diesel at El Segundo refinery and completed sale of Papa-Terra oil field interest in Brazil.
macro_exposures: Operations remain exposed to crude oil and natural gas price volatility subject to OPEC+ production curtailments and other producing country actions. COVID-19 variants continue impacting logistics and materials movement with project delays pushing first oil timelines. Rising commodity and raw material costs create inflationary pressures on industry inputs as drilling activity accelerates.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued upward pressure on oil and gas industry input costs correlated with rising rig counts. The company maintains commitment to 2028 upstream production greenhouse gas intensity targets and energy transition strategy investments including renewable natural gas through California CNG stations by 2025. Full-year pension plan contributions of approximately $1.75 billion are expected.
uncertainty_profile: Project contingency increased recognizing schedule uncertainty from COVID-19 variants. Multiple climate change-related lawsuits filed by governmental entities seek damages and injunctive relief against fossil fuel operations with potential for material adverse effects. The fraudulent Ecuador judgment remains unenforceable following international arbitration rulings with Louisiana coastal erosion lawsuits under unprecedented legal theories presenting uncertain scope of potential damages.
